title: AitpRevocationList
description: AITP v0.1 signed revocation snapshot — the response served by an issuing peer's `ListRevoked` HTTPS endpoint (RFC-AITP-0008 §1.5). Lists every TCT JTI the issuer has revoked. Cached by consuming peers per `expires_at`; an empty `entries` array is itself a meaningful signed assertion that nothing has been revoked since the last snapshot, defending against an attacker that suppresses fresher snapshots to roll back revocations.

issuer.description: The issuing peer's AID. MUST equal the `issuer` of every TCT covered by `entries`.

published_at.description: Unix timestamp when this snapshot was signed.

expires_at.description: Unix timestamp after which this snapshot MUST NOT be cached.

revoked_at.description: Unix timestamp when the issuing peer revoked this TCT.

reason.description: Optional human-readable reason. Not used in trust decisions.

entries.description: Revoked-entry records. MAY be empty — an empty list is itself a signed assertion that nothing has been revoked, defending against suppression of fresher snapshots.

signature.description: Unpadded base64url signature (RFC 4648 §5) by the issuing peer over the JCS-canonical bytes of `revocation_list` (RFC 8785), excluding this field. See RFC-AITP-0001 §5.4. The known-answer body at `schemas/conformance/known-answer/jcs-sha256.json` (`kat-revocation-001`) pins the canonical bytes; a runtime-served snapshot MUST also carry this field.
